*   `DELETE /api/Project/{id}` → Delete project. Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
*   `GET /api/Project/{id}/tasks` → All tasks for a project. Explicit columns with `::timestamp` casting.
*   `POST /api/Project/tasks` → Create and assign a task. Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
    *   Body: `{ projectId, assignedToEmpId, taskTitle, taskDescription, taskStatus, priority, startDate, dueDate, workingHours }`
    *   Defaults: `taskStatus = "Pending"`, `priority = "Medium"`, `createdat = NOW()`
*   `PUT /api/Project/tasks/{taskId}` → Full task update. Auth: Admin, TeamLead. Auto-stamps `completedat = NOW()` if status = "Completed".
*   `PATCH /api/Project/tasks/{taskId}/status` → Status-only update.
    *   Admin/TL/Manager can update any task.
    *   Employee can ONLY update tasks assigned to them — verified by fetching their tasks and checking if taskId is in the list.
    *   Body: `{ status: "Pending" | "InProgress" | "Completed" }`
*   `GET /api/Project/my-tasks` → Tasks assigned to the logged-in user (any role). JOINs `t_projects` to include `projectname`. Debug: logs EmpId and count to console.
*   `GET /api/Project/employee/{empid}/tasks` → Admin/TL/Manager can fetch any employee's tasks. Employee can only fetch own.

### Repository Queries (`ProjectRepository.cs`)

*   **GetTasksByProjectIdAsync**: Explicit column SELECT with `startdate::timestamp`, `duedate::timestamp` casting. Ordered by `createdat DESC`.
*   **GetTasksByEmployeeIdAsync**: JOINs `t_projects` on `projectid` to include `p.projectname`. Explicit column listing with timestamp casting. Ordered by `t.createdat DESC`.
*   **CreateTaskAsync**: Uses `NOW()` server-side instead of `@CreatedAt` C# parameter. Returns `taskid` via `RETURNING`.
*   **UpdateTaskAsync**: Sets `completedat = NOW()` when status = "Completed", preserves existing `completedat` otherwise.
*   **UpdateTaskStatusAsync**: Same `completedat` auto-stamp logic for status-only updates.
*   **GetProjectsByEmpIdAsync**: JOINs `t_projecttasks` to find projects where employee has tasks. Falls back to all projects if none found.
*   **GetProjectsByCreator**: Filters `WHERE createdbyid = @EmpId` for TL's "My Projects" tab.

### Models

*   **Project**: `ProjectId`, `CreatedById`, `ProjectName`, `Description`, `Links[]`, `DocumentationLinks[]`, `StartDate`, `EndDate`, `TeamId`, `CreatedAt`
*   **ProjectTask**: `TaskId`, `ProjectId`, `AssignedToEmpId`, `TaskTitle`, `TaskDescription`, `TaskStatus` (Pending/InProgress/Completed), `Priority` (Low/Medium/High/Critical), `StartDate`, `DueDate`, `CompletedAt`, `WorkingHours`, `EstimatedHours`, `ProjectName` (joined), `CreatedAt`

### Frontend (`ProjectsPage.js`)

#### Role-Based Tab Structure
*   **TeamLead** (`canManage = true`): 3 tabs — My Projects | All Assigned Tasks | My Work
*   **Manager**: 2 tabs — All Projects (read-only) | My Assigned Tasks
*   **Employee**: 2 tabs — My Projects | My Tasks

#### State Variables
*   `tab`: `'myProjects'` | `'allTasks'` | `'myWork'`
*   `projects`: Array loaded based on role (TL: `getMyProjects()`, others: `getProjects()`)
*   `myTasks`: Array from `GET /api/Project/my-tasks` — powers "My Work"/"My Tasks" tab
*   `selectedProject`: Currently expanded project in the split view
*   `projectTasks`: Tasks for the selected project
*   `teamMembers`: Active employees for task assignment dropdown (TL/Manager only)
*   `myTaskIds`: `Set` of task IDs from `myTasks` — used to flag "MINE" badges and enable status cycling

#### On Page Load (3 parallel fetches)
1. `loadProjects()` → TL calls `getMyProjects()`, others call `getProjects()`
2. `loadMyTasks()` → ALL roles call `getMyTasks()` with debug logging
3. `loadTeamMembers()` → TL/Manager calls `GET /api/User/team` → filters to `status === 'active'`

#### My Projects / All Projects Tab
*   Split view: project list (340px) | task panel (when project selected)
*   Clicking a project card fetches `GET /api/Project/{id}/tasks` → shows task panel
*   TL sees "Assign Task" button on each project card and in the task panel header
*   Project links rendered from `"Name|URL"` pipe-separated strings as clickable badges

#### All Assigned Tasks Tab (TL only)
*   `AllTasksView` component fires `Promise.allSettled()` across ALL TL's projects
*   Fetches tasks for each project in parallel via `GET /api/Project/{id}/tasks`
*   Flattens all tasks into a single list, each tagged with `_projectName`
*   Each task shows a purple project name badge above it
*   Status cycling enabled only for tasks also assigned to the TL

#### My Work / My Tasks Tab
*   Displays all tasks from `myTasks` array (fetched via `GET /api/Project/my-tasks`)
*   All tasks have `isMyTask = true`, `canUpdateStatus = true`
*   Status badge click cycles: Pending → InProgress → Completed → Pending
*   Calls `PATCH /api/Project/tasks/{taskId}/status`
*   Optimistic UI update: both `projectTasks` and `myTasks` arrays updated client-side on success

#### TaskCard Component
*   Displays: task title, "MINE" badge, description, status badge (color-coded), priority badge (color-coded), due date, estimated hours
*   Status colors: Completed (green), InProgress (blue), Pending (yellow), Todo (gray)
*   Priority colors: High (red), Medium (amber), Low (green), Critical (purple)
*   `cycleStatus()`: only fires when `canUpdateStatus = true`, cycles Pending→InProgress→Completed→Pending

#### AssignTaskModal
*   Triggered by TL clicking "Assign Task"
*   Form: Assign To (active team members dropdown), Task Title, Description, Priority, Status, Start Date, Due Date, Est. Hours
*   Employee dropdown shows: `{name || email prefix} ({role}) — #{empId}`
*   On submit: `POST /api/Project/tasks` → reloads myTasks + re-opens project task panel

#### CreateProjectModal
*   Form: Project Name, Description, Start/End Date, Team ID, Project Links (dynamic rows), Documentation Links (dynamic rows)
*   Links serialized as `"Name|URL"` before submit, empty rows filtered out

### Frontend API Client (`projects.js`)
*   `getProjects()` → `GET /Project`
*   `getMyProjects()` → `GET /Project/my`
*   `createProject(data)` → `POST /Project`
*   `updateProject(id, data)` → `PUT /Project/{id}`
*   `deleteProject(id)` → `DELETE /Project/{id}`
*   `getTasks(projectId)` → `GET /Project/{projectId}/tasks`
*   `assignTask(data)` → `POST /Project/tasks`
*   `updateTask(taskId, data)` → `PUT /Project/tasks/{taskId}`
*   `updateTaskStatus(taskId, status)` → `PATCH /Project/tasks/{taskId}/status`
*   `getMyTasks()` → `GET /Project/my-tasks`
*   `getEmployeeTasks(empId)` → `GET /Project/employee/{empId}/tasks`

### Permission Matrix

| Action | Admin | TeamLead | Manager | Employee |
|--------|-------|----------|---------|----------|
| See all projects | ✅ | ❌ (own) | ✅ (read-only) | ❌ (assigned) |
| Create project | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete project | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Assign tasks | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update any task status | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Update own task status | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| See "All Assigned Tasks" tab | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| See "My Work/Tasks" tab | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

### Critical Debug Checklist
*   If "My Work" tab shows empty → check backend console for `[my-tasks] Logged in EmpId: X` and `Tasks Count: Y`
*   If count = 0 → the `assignedtoempid` in `t_projecttasks` doesn't match the logged-in user's `empid`
*   Verify: `SELECT * FROM t_projecttasks` → check `assignedtoempid` values match `SELECT empid FROM t_users WHERE email = '...'`
*   Frontend console should show `[ProjectsPage] MY TASKS: [...]` — if empty array, issue is backend; if error, check network tab

---

## 📂 13. Project & Task Management V2 — AdminId Linking + Batch Creation + Search

### What Changed (V2 Upgrade)

1. **AdminId on Projects**: Every project is now linked to a company via `adminid` (resolved from user → space → admin). Admin sees only their company's projects via `WHERE adminid = @EmpId` instead of all projects globally.
2. **Create Project With Tasks (Batch API)**: New `POST /api/Project/create-with-tasks` endpoint creates a project AND its tasks in a single API call. Frontend modal supports inline task creation.
3. **Dedicated All Assigned Tasks API**: New `GET /api/Project/all-assigned-tasks?search=` endpoint fetches tasks from projects created by the TL using a single SQL query with JOIN, replacing the old client-side `Promise.allSettled()` approach.
4. **Search Bar**: Backend supports `LIKE` search on task title + project name. Frontend has a search bar in the "All Assigned Tasks" tab.
5. **Solid Modal UI**: All modals now use solid white backgrounds with gradient purple headers instead of transparent/glassmorphism. Inputs are properly styled with borders and backgrounds.

### Database Migration (Program.cs)

```sql
ALTER TABLE t_projects ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS adminid INTEGER;
```

Runs automatically on startup via the existing migration block in Program.cs.

### Models

**Project** (`Models/Project.cs`):
*   `ProjectId`, `CreatedById`, **`AdminId`** (NEW), `ProjectName`, `Description`, `Links[]`, `DocumentationLinks[]`, `StartDate`, `EndDate`, `TeamId`, `CreatedAt`

**ProjectTask** (`Models/ProjectTask.cs`):
*   `TaskId`, `ProjectId`, `AssignedToEmpId`, `TaskTitle`, `TaskDescription`, `TaskStatus`, `Priority`, `StartDate`, `DueDate`, `CompletedAt`, `WorkingHours`, `EstimatedHours`, `ProjectName` (joined), `CreatedAt`

**CreateProjectWithTasksDto** (NEW — in `Models/Project.cs`):
*   `ProjectName`, `Description`, `Links[]`, `DocumentationLinks[]`, `StartDate`, `EndDate`, `TeamId`
*   `Tasks`: List of `TaskDto` — each has: `AssignedToEmpId`, `TaskTitle`, `TaskDescription`, `Priority`, `StartDate`, `DueDate`, `WorkingHours`

### Backend API Endpoints (`ProjectController.cs`)

**DI Dependencies**: `IProjectRepository`, `IUserRepository`, `ISpaceRepository`

*   `GET /api/Project` → Role-based:
    *   **Admin** → `GetProjectsByAdminIdAsync(empId)` — only company projects
    *   **Manager** → resolves adminId via user→space→admin chain, then `GetProjectsByAdminIdAsync(adminId)`
    *   **Employee/TL** → `GetProjectsByEmpIdAsync(empId)` — projects where they have tasks
*   `GET /api/Project/my` → TL's created projects (`WHERE createdbyid = @EmpId`). Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
*   `GET /api/Project/{id}` → Single project by ID.

*   **`POST /api/Project`** → Create project. Auto-resolves `adminId`:
    *   If role=Admin → `adminId = empId`
    *   Else → fetch user → fetch space → `adminId = space.AdminId`

*   **`POST /api/Project/create-with-tasks`** (NEW) → Batch creation:
    *   Body: `CreateProjectWithTasksDto` (project details + tasks array)
    *   Creates project first, then loops through tasks and creates each one
    *   Auto-resolves adminId same as above
    *   Returns: `{ projectId, projectName, tasksCreated, tasks: [{ taskId, taskTitle, assignedToEmpId }] }`
    *   Auth: Admin, TeamLead

*   `PUT /api/Project/{id}` → Update project. Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
*   `DELETE /api/Project/{id}` → Delete project. Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
*   `GET /api/Project/{id}/tasks` → All tasks for a project (now includes JOIN for projectname).
*   `POST /api/Project/tasks` → Assign single task. Auth: Admin, TeamLead.
*   `PUT /api/Project/tasks/{taskId}` → Full task update.
*   `PATCH /api/Project/tasks/{taskId}/status` → Status-only update. Employee verified against own tasks.

*   **`GET /api/Project/all-assigned-tasks?search=`** (NEW) → All tasks from TL's projects:
    *   No search → `GetTasksByCreatorAsync(empId)` — JOIN `t_projects WHERE p.createdbyid = @EmpId`
    *   With search → `SearchTasksByCreatorAsync(empId, search)` — adds `LIKE` filter on tasktitle + projectname
    *   Auth: Admin, TeamLead
    *   Console log: `[all-assigned-tasks] EmpId=X, Search='Y', Count=Z`
    | Progress Report | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Queries | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Leaves | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Spaces | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
    | Profile | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

    ---

    ## PAYROLL / CTC API DEBUG REPORT
    ### Date: 2026-05-25

    ### Issue: GET /api/Salary/me and GET /api/payroll/myslips returning 404

    ---

    ### ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS

    #### ISSUE 1 — `dotnet watch` Stale Process (PRIMARY CAUSE OF 404s)
    - **File**: Backend running on port 5125
    - **Cause**: `dotnet watch` detected file changes (ISalaryRepository + SalaryRepository + PayrollController) but could NOT hot-reload because `Backend.exe` (PID 48388) held a file lock on the output DLL.
    - **Symptom**: Old compiled code continued running — routes from NEW files (`/api/payroll/myslips`) were NOT registered. The process entered a broken state where Swagger itself returned 500.
    - **Fix**: **Stop `dotnet watch` → restart it** from the Backend folder. This forces a clean rebuild.

    #### ISSUE 2 — Request Deduplication Bug in client.js (CAUSED 4 REPEATED REQUESTS)
    - **File**: `src/api/client.js` lines 31–43
    - **Cause**: The deduplication interceptor created a NEW AbortController for every GET request and set `config.signal` to it. When SalaryPage fired 7 parallel requests via `Promise.allSettled`, the interceptor treated each new request as a "duplicate" of the previous one and aborted them.
    - **Fix**: Rewrote dedup logic to only cancel the NEW duplicate (not the existing one), and to NOT override the caller-provided signal.

    #### ISSUE 3 — No salary record for new employees (CAUSED "No salary data available")
    - **File**: `Backend/Program.cs`
    - **Cause**: `t_employeesalary` table existed but had no rows for newly registered employees. `GetSalaryAsync` had a fallback (role-based defaults) but only if `t_employeesalary` didn't have a row. Race condition: the table existed but the employee row didn't.
    - **Fix**: Added auto-seed on startup that inserts a default salary for every Active user without an existing row.

    #### ISSUE 4 — Employee payslip breakdown never visible (MISSING ENDPOINT)
    - **File**: `PayrollController.cs`
    - **Cause**: No endpoint existed to fetch from `t_payslips`. Employees had no way to see the admin-configured breakdown.
    - **Fix**: Added `GET /api/payroll/myslips` endpoint + `GetMyPayslipsAsync` in SalaryRepository.

    ---

    ### FIXES APPLIED

    | File | Change |
    |------|--------|
    | `src/api/client.js` | Fixed dedup interceptor — no longer overwrites caller signal |
    | `Backend/Program.cs` | Added salary auto-seed for employees without salary records |
    | `Backend/Controllers/PayrollController.cs` | Added GET /api/payroll/myslips |
    | `Backend/Repositories/ISalaryRepository.cs` | Added GetMyPayslipsAsync interface method |
    | `Backend/Repositories/SalaryRepository.cs` | Implemented GetMyPayslipsAsync |
    | `src/api/payroll.js` | Added getMyPayslips() API wrapper |
    | `src/pages/SalaryPage.js` | Added "My Payslips" tab showing admin-configured breakdown |

    ---

    ### RESTART REQUIRED

    **Stop and restart** the `dotnet watch` terminal:
    ```
    Ctrl+C  (in the backend terminal)
    dotnet watch
    ```

    This forces the backend to rebuild with all new code. After restart, `/api/Salary/me` and `/api/payroll/myslips` will work correctly.

    ---

    ### VERIFIED ROUTE MAP (After Restart)

    | Endpoint | Controller | Auth |
    |----------|-----------|------|
    | GET /api/Salary/me?month=&year= | SalaryController | [Authorize] (all roles) |
    | GET /api/payroll/history | PayrollController | [Authorize] (all roles) |
    | GET /api/payroll/myslips | PayrollController | [Authorize] (all roles) |
    | GET /api/payroll/ctc-summary?year= | PayrollController | [Authorize] (all roles) |
    | GET /api/payroll/impact | AnalyticsController | [Authorize] (all roles) |
    | GET /api/performance | AnalyticsController | [Authorize] (all roles) |

*   `GET /api/Project/my-tasks` → Tasks assigned TO the logged-in user. Debug logged.
*   `GET /api/Project/employee/{empid}/tasks` → Admin/TL/Manager can fetch any employee's tasks.

### Repository Methods (`ProjectRepository.cs`)

**New Methods:**
*   `GetProjectsByAdminIdAsync(int adminId)` — `WHERE adminid = @AdminId` — for Admin company view
*   `GetTasksByCreatorAsync(int empId)` — `JOIN t_projects WHERE p.createdbyid = @EmpId` — for All Assigned Tasks
*   `SearchTasksByCreatorAsync(int empId, string search)` — same JOIN + `LIKE` filter on tasktitle/projectname

**Updated Methods:**
*   `CreateProjectAsync` — now includes `adminid` in INSERT
*   `GetTasksByProjectIdAsync` — now JOINs `t_projects` to include `projectname`
*   All SELECT queries include `adminid` in column list

### AdminId Resolution Flow

```
TL clicks "Create Project"
    → Controller gets EmpId from JWT
    → Fetches User by EmpId → gets SpaceId
    → Fetches Space by SpaceId → gets AdminId
    → Sets project.AdminId = space.AdminId
    → INSERT INTO t_projects ... adminid = @AdminId

Admin clicks "Create Project"
    → AdminId = their own EmpId (they ARE the admin)
```

### Frontend API Client (`projects.js`)

**New Functions:**
*   `createProjectWithTasks(data)` → `POST /Project/create-with-tasks`
*   `getAllAssignedTasks(search, signal)` → `GET /Project/all-assigned-tasks?search=`

### Frontend (`ProjectsPage.js`)

#### Key Changes from V1
1. **`canManage`** now includes Admin: `const canManage = isTL || isAdmin`
2. **Admin tab label**: "Company Projects" instead of "My Projects"
3. **All Assigned Tasks** uses dedicated API instead of client-side aggregation
4. **Search bar** in All Assigned Tasks tab — real-time backend search
5. **TaskCard** now shows `projectName` badge on every task (data comes from JOIN)
6. **Status updates** also update `allAssignedTasks` state array

#### State Variables (New/Changed)
*   `allAssignedTasks` → Array from `GET /all-assigned-tasks` — replaces old `AllTasksView` component
*   `loadingAssigned` → Loading state for All Assigned Tasks
*   `searchQuery` → Current search input value

#### All Assigned Tasks Tab (tab = 'allTasks')
*   On tab switch → calls `loadAllAssignedTasks(searchQuery)`
*   Search form: input + Search button + Clear button
*   Results show task count + search context message
*   Each task rendered via `TaskCard` with `canUpdateStatus = true` for TL/Admin
*   No more `Promise.allSettled()` — single API call

#### Create Project Modal (Upgraded)
*   Solid white background with gradient purple header
*   **Inline task creation**: "Tasks (N)" section at bottom with "Add Task" button
*   Each task row: Task Title, Assign To (dropdown), Priority, Est. Hours, Description
*   On submit with tasks → uses `createProjectWithTasks()` batch API
*   On submit without tasks → uses regular `createProject()` API
*   Success toast shows task count: "✅ Project created with 3 tasks!"

#### Assign Task Modal (Upgraded)
*   Same solid white + gradient purple header style
*   Inputs have proper white backgrounds with borders (not transparent)

#### Modal Styling
```javascript
modalOverlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.6) + backdropFilter blur(4px)
modalCard: #fff background, borderRadius 16, boxShadow 0 25px 60px
modalHeader: linear-gradient(135deg, #4F46E5, #7C3AED), white text
inputStyle: white background, 1px solid #D1D5DB, borderRadius 10
```

### Permission Matrix (V2)

| Action | Admin | TeamLead | Manager | Employee |
|--------|-------|----------|---------|----------|
| See company projects | ✅ (adminid) | ❌ (own) | ✅ (via adminid) | ❌ (assigned) |
| Create project | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Create project + tasks | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Delete project | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Assign tasks | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| See "All Assigned Tasks" | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Search assigned tasks | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Update any task status | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Update own task status | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| See "My Work/Tasks" | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

### Critical Debug Checklist (V2)
*   Backend console: `[create-with-tasks] Project #X created with Y tasks by EmpId=Z, AdminId=W`
*   Backend console: `[all-assigned-tasks] EmpId=X, Search='Y', Count=Z`
*   If Admin sees no projects → check `adminid` column exists in `t_projects` and is populated
*   If All Assigned Tasks is empty → check `t_projects.createdbyid` matches the TL's empid
*   Run: `SELECT p.projectid, p.projectname, p.createdbyid, p.adminid FROM t_projects p` to verify linkage

## ?? 12. Core Module Architecture: Productivity, Performance & Payroll Impact

### API Endpoints (AnalyticsController.cs & LeaveController.cs)
*   GET /api/analytics/productivity ? Calculates composite score from attendance, worklogs, and tasks.
*   GET /api/payroll/impact ? Derives net salary taking late/early penalties into account via hourly rate deductions.
*   GET /api/performance ? Maps productivity score to A+, A, B, C, D grading.
*   POST /api/Leave ? Apply for leave.
*   GET /api/Leave/me ? Employee's leave history.
*   PATCH /api/Leave/{id}/status ? Approve/reject leaves.

### Database Additions
*   **	_attendance**: Added overtimehours NUMERIC(5,2) to track hours beyond expected space work hours.
*   **	_leaves**: leaveid, empid, leavedate, reason, status for leave tracking.

### Logic Details
*   **Productivity**: 30% Attendance + 40% Worklogs + 30% Task Completion.
*   **Auto-Mark Leave**: Analytics engine intercepts Approved leaves and subtracts them from Absent Days to mitigate penalty automatically.

---

## 📊 13. Working Logs Chart Module

### API Endpoints (`WorklogController.cs`)
*   `GET /api/worklogs/me?range=weekly|monthly|6months` → Fetches the employee's logged hours, break hours, before-break, and after-break hours aggregated by the selected range.

### Backend Calculation Logic (`WorklogRepository.cs`)
*   **Daily Base Mapping**:
    *   Iterates through each date in the range.
    *   Determines `ExpectedHours`: 0 on weekends or days prior to the employee's `DateOfJoining` (to prevent incorrect missing hours); otherwise, defaults to the space's standard working hours (usually 8h).
    *   `BeforeBreak`: Hours worked before the first break (calculated as `FirstBreakStart - ClockIn`). If no break exists, it defaults to the full clocked duration (up to standard 8h).
    *   `Break`: Total break minutes from `employeebreaks` converted to hours (or fallback to `BreakHours` in `t_attendance`).
    *   `AfterBreak`: Remaining logged hours (`TotalLoggedHours - BeforeBreak`).
    *   `Missing`: Unlogged hours needed to meet the expected hours for that day (`MAX(0, ExpectedHours - TotalLoggedHours)`).
*   **Interval Aggregation**:
    *   **weekly**: Groups into standard days (Mon-Sun) of the current week.
    *   **monthly**: Groups by each individual date (1-31) of the current month.
    *   **6months**: Groups and sums hours into the last 6 calendar months.

### Frontend Custom Component (`EmployeeDashboard.js` - `<WorkingLogsChart />`)
*   **Custom Stacked Bar Rendering**: Built using React and dynamic flexbox/div layout (no external charting libraries) for compatibility with React 19.
*   **Interactive Legend**: Allows toggle filters for `beforeBreak`, `break`, `afterBreak`, and `missing` categories. Checking/unchecking a category recalculates the layout height dynamically.
*   **Dynamic Scaling**: Finds the maximum sum for any day in the current dataset (using a minimum baseline of 8) and scales bars to fit within a `160px` tall responsive chart area.
*   **Interactive Tooltip**: Hovering over a bar displays detailed category durations and a final comparison of worked hours.
*   **Clean X-Axis Labels**: For monthly view, labels are conditionally thinned (showing every 5th tick) to prevent overlapping on mobile screens.

---

## 🔐 RBAC Refactor Change Log — 2026-05-25

### Summary
Fixed role-based access control issues, API mismatches, and empty data problems. UI/layout unchanged.

### Changes Made

#### `Frontend/react-crud/src/App.js`
- **`/admin/users` route**: Changed `allowedRoles` from `['Admin', 'TeamLead', 'Manager']` → `['Admin']` only.
  - Reason: Managers and TeamLeads should NOT have access to the admin employee management page.
  - Security: ProtectedRoute now redirects unauthorized users to `/employee`.

#### `Frontend/react-crud/src/components/AppLayout.js`
- **`MANAGER_EXTRA_NAV`**: Removed the `{ path: '/admin/users', icon: 'manage_accounts', label: 'Employee Mgmt' }` entry.
  - Reason: Manager had a direct sidebar link to an Admin-only route — this was the navigation RBAC bug.
  - Manager now only gets: `{ path: '/employee/all-employees', label: 'All Employees' }` (read-only directory).
  - TeamLead keeps `{ path: '/employee/all-employees', label: 'Team Members' }`.
  - UI design (sidebar structure, icons, animations, colors) is completely unchanged.

#### `Frontend/react-crud/src/pages/UsersPage.js`
- **Role-conditional API**: Added `fetchUsers()` helper — Admin calls `GET /api/User`, others call `GET /api/User/company`.
  - Reason: Manager was calling Admin-only `/api/User` and getting 403 or empty data.
- **Dynamic page title**: Admin sees "Employee Management"; Manager/TL sees "Team Members".
- **Empty-state guard**: Explicitly sets `setUsers([])` when `data` is null/empty, preventing stale state.
- **AbortController**: `useEffect` now uses `AbortController` for proper cleanup on unmount.
- **All reload calls** (add, edit, status update) updated to use role-conditional `fetchUsers()`.

#### `Backend/Controllers/UserController.cs`
- **`GET /api/User`**: Changed from `[Authorize(Roles = "Admin,TeamLead,Manager")]` → `[Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]`.
  - Reason: This endpoint is the admin-scoped full user list. Only Admin should call it.
- **`GET /api/User/company`**: Changed from `[Authorize]` → `[Authorize(Roles = "Admin,Manager,TeamLead,Employee")]`.
  - Reason: Explicit role list is clearer and more secure than open `[Authorize]`.
  - This is the safe company-directory endpoint that all roles can read.

#### `Backend/Repositories/UserRepository.cs` — `GetUsersByCompanyAsync`
- **Critical bug fixed**: Original query used `INNER JOIN t_users u2 ON u2.spaceid = s2.spaceid WHERE u2.empid = @EmpId` — this fails when the user's `spaceid` is NULL (common for Admin users before their spaceid was back-filled).
- **Fix**: Replaced with `COALESCE(Path1, Path2)` query:
  - **Path 1**: Employee has spaceid → resolve adminid through t_spaces JOIN (normal path for Manager/TL/Employee)
  - **Path 2**: User IS an admin → match empid directly as adminid in t_spaces (covers Admin whose spaceid may be null)
- **Result**: Manager/TL/Employee now correctly see all employees in their company; Admin sees all company employees even when their own spaceid is null.

### Role Access Matrix (After Fix)

| Route | Admin | Manager | TeamLead | Employee |
|-------|-------|---------|----------|----------|
| `/admin` | ✅ | ❌→/employee | ❌→/employee | ❌→/employee |
| `/admin/users` | ✅ | ❌→/employee | ❌→/employee | ❌→/employee |
| `/employee/all-employees` | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| `GET /api/User` | ✅ | ❌ 403 | ❌ 403 | ❌ 403 |
| `GET /api/User/company` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

### Sidebar Items Per Role (After Fix)

| Item | Admin | Manager | TeamLead | Employee |
|------|-------|---------|----------|----------|
| Dashboard | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Employee Mgmt (`/admin/users`) | ✅ | ❌ removed | ❌ | ❌ |
| All Employees (`/employee/all-employees`) | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ (as "Team Members") | ❌ |
| Attendance | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Projects | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Work Logs | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Payroll / CTC | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Progress Report | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Queries | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Leaves | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Spaces | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Profile | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

### 7. Payroll Payouts & Payslips Upgrade (May 2026)

- **Manual Payment Methods**: Added `Bank Transfer` to the checkout system, complementing the manual `Cash` and `UPI` routes. 
- **Bank Payout Routing & Profile Verification**: Updated `ValidatePayoutProfile` (in `SpaceController.cs`) to ensure employees being paid via `Bank Transfer` have complete coordinates configured in their profile (Account Number, Bank Name, Holder Name, and IFSC Code), identical to the online `Razorpay` pre-requisites.
- **Bulk Payout & Transaction IDs**: Enabled admins to record custom transaction/reference IDs for `Bank Transfer` payouts in `PayrollPage.js`, with automatic batch status verification.
- **Payslip & PDF Generation**: 
  - Enabled employees to download a high-fidelity, beautifully styled PDF copy of their payslips from `SalaryPage.js`. 
  - Integrated `useAuth` hook in `PayslipCard` to dynamically output employee name. 
  - Injected an Outfit font-supported invoice layout that handles itemized Gross Earnings, Deductions (including attendance-based warning penalties), Net Pay, and the selected payment method details cleanly.

---

## 🎥 14. Live Screen Monitoring Module (June 2026)

### Database Models (`Models/Screenshot.cs`)
*   **`EmployeeScreenshotLog`**: Added `EmployeeName`, `UserSpaceId`, `UserEmpId`, and `UserEmail` properties to query user information with screenshot data.
*   **`EmployeeVideoLog`**: Added similar fields to map user metadata to video logs.
*   **`EmployeeLatestScreenshot`**: Extended with `Email` and `Status` fields.

### Backend Controller & API Contracts (`MonitorController.cs` & `ScreenshotService.cs`)
*   `GET /api/Monitor/space/{spaceId}/latest` -> Returns the latest screenshot logs for all employees in the space. Checks physical file existence inside `wwwroot` and sets `status = "available" | "missing"`.
*   `GET /api/Monitor/employee-activity` -> Structured endpoint returning history of screenshots and videos for a specific employee context (`empid`, `spaceid`). Validates physical file existence on disk for both screenshots (JPEG) and recordings (WEBM), returning `status = "available" | "missing"`.
*   **Space Leakage Prevention**: All logs endpoints check the caller's JWT space context or space filters to prevent unauthorized access to employee activity logs of other space IDs.
*   **Title-Cased Email Fallback**: When loading employee history or latest snapshots, if the employee name in `t_users` is empty/null, the backend fallback-parses a Title-Cased name derived from their email username (e.g. `test@manageremp.com` becomes `Test`) to avoid "Unknown Employee" labels.

### Storage Persistence Configuration (`docker-compose.yml`)
*   Binds host folder `./Backend/wwwroot` to container `/app/wwwroot`. This ensures that captured screenshots and video logs are saved persistently on the host machine and are not lost during container builds or restarts.

### Frontend UI & Fallback Placeholders (`LiveMonitoringPage.js`)
*   **`getFullUrl` Helper**: Normalizes URLs dynamically so that leading slashes are handled safely, avoiding double slashes or missing slashes when prepending `BACKEND_ORIGIN`.
*   **Main Feed Grid**: If `feed.status` is `missing`, displays a clean visual card stating `"Screenshot unavailable (file missing)"`. If no screenshot is logged, displays `"No captures logged"`.
*   **History Modal (Screenshots & Recordings)**: If `status === 'missing'`, it hides the broken `<img>` or `<video>` element and renders a dashed placeholder card stating `"Screenshot unavailable (file missing)"` or `"Recording unavailable (file missing)"` respectively, while keeping the interactive timestamp footer visible and functional.
